content:
# Revelation Chapter 22 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Codex Sinaiticus, pages 294-295 | **Source Image:** REV_022_page_001.jpg, REV_022_page_002.jpg

1. And he showed me a river of the water of life, bright as crystal, proceeding out of the throne of God and of the Lamb.

2. In the midst of its street, and on this side and on that side of the river, was the tree of life producing twelve fruits, yielding its fruit according to each month; and the leaves of the tree were for the healing of the nations.

3. And there shall be no more curse. And the throne of God and of the Lamb shall be in it, and his servants shall serve him,

4. and they shall see his face, and his name shall be upon their foreheads.

5. And night shall be no more, and they have no need of the light of a lamp and the light of the sun, for the Lord God shall shine upon them, and they shall reign unto the ages of the ages.

6. And he said to me, "These words are faithful and true. And the Lord, the God of the spirits of the prophets, sent his angel to show his servants the things which must come to pass shortly.

7. And behold, I am coming quickly. Blessed is the one who keeps the words of the prophecy of this book."

8. And I, John, am the one hearing and seeing these things. And when I heard and saw, I fell down to worship before the feet of the angel who showed me these things.

9. And he says to me, "See that you do not! I am a fellow servant of you and of your brothers the prophets and of those who keep the words of this book. Worship God."

10. And he says to me, "Do not seal the words of the prophecy of this book, for the time is near.

11. Let the one who does wrong continue to do wrong, and let the filthy one continue to be filthy, and let the righteous one continue to practice righteousness, and let the holy one continue to be holy."

12. "Behold, I am coming quickly, and my reward is with me, to render to each one as his work is.

13. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end."

14. Blessed are those who wash their robes, so that their authority shall be over the tree of life, and they may enter by the gates into the city.

15. Outside are the dogs, and the sorcerers, and the sexually immoral, and the murderers, and the idolaters, and everyone who loves and practices falsehood.

16. "I, Jesus, sent my angel to testify these things to you for the churches. I am the root and the offspring of David, the bright morning star."

17. And the Spirit and the bride say, "Come." And let the one who hears say, "Come." And let the one who thirsts come; let the one who desires take the water of life freely.

18. I testify to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy of this book: if anyone adds to them, God shall add to him the plagues that are written in this book.

19. And if anyone takes away from the words of the book of this prophecy, God shall take away his part from the tree of life and out of the holy city, the things which are written in this book.

20. The one who testifies these things says, "Yes, I am coming quickly." Amen. Come, Lord Jesus.

21. The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all the saints. Amen.

---
## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**v.4** -- All three translators agreed: "they shall see his face, and his name shall be upon their foreheads." This is the Bible's ultimate promise of unmediated communion -- where once no one could see God's face and live (Exodus 33:20), here the redeemed behold him directly.

**v.7** -- All three translators agreed on the beatitude: "Blessed is the one who keeps the words of the prophecy of this book."

**v.10** -- All three translators agreed: "Do not seal the words of the prophecy of this book, for the time is near."

**v.13** -- All three translators agreed on the threefold title: "I am the Alpha and the Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end."

**v.14** -- All three translators agreed on the Sinaiticus reading "wash their robes" rather than the Textus Receptus "do his commandments" -- a significant textual decision faithfully grounded in the manuscript.

**v.19** -- All three translators agreed on the Sinaiticus reading "tree of life" rather than the later variant "book of life" -- another major textual decision. The punishment for tampering is loss of access to the tree and the holy city, not erasure from a book.

**v.20** -- All three translators preserved the structure: the testifier's promise ("Yes, I am coming quickly"), the responsive "Amen," and the closing prayer "Come, Lord Jesus." This is the last prayer of the Bible -- an echo of the ancient Aramaic *Maranatha*.

**A note on the closing of Scripture:** Verse 21 is the final line of the New Testament in Codex Sinaiticus. The manuscript concludes with AMHN, followed by the colophon APOKALYPSIS IOANNOU ("Revelation of John"). The Bible ends not with thunder or judgment but with a benediction of grace, offered to all the saints, sealed with Amen. The manuscript page -- the last of the canonical text in Sinaiticus -- is visible in REV_022_page_002.jpg, where the closing words and the colophon marking IOANNOU can be clearly seen.

---
## Disagreement Log

### Verse 1
- **EMET:** "a river of water of life, bright as crystal"
- **LOGOS:** "a river of the water of life, bright as crystal"
- **PNEUMA:** "the river of the water of life, sparkling like crystal"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. The article before "water" (*tou hydatos*) is present in Sinaiticus. PNEUMA's "sparkling" dynamically renders *lampron* ("bright/radiant"), and "the river" adds a definite article where the Greek has *potamon* without the article.

### Verse 2
- **EMET:** "In [the] middle of her street"
- **LOGOS:** "In the midst of its street"
- **PNEUMA:** "down the middle of the city's great street"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "city's great" which is not in the Greek. EMET's "her" reflects the feminine Greek but English convention uses "its."

### Verse 2 (continued)
- **EMET:** "[was the] tree of life producing twelve fruits, according to each month yielding its fruit"
- **LOGOS:** "was a tree of life producing twelve fruits, yielding its fruit according to each month"
- **PNEUMA:** "stood the tree of life, bearing twelve kinds of fruit -- yielding a fresh crop every month"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "kinds of" (interpretive for *karpous dōdeka*), "fresh crop" (not in the Greek), and "stood" where the Greek implies "was." The article before "tree" is debated; SOPHIA follows LOGOS's "a tree" for v.2 while noting the singular is preserved.

### Verse 2 (final clause)
- **EMET:** "the leaves of the tree [are] for [the] healing of the nations"
- **LOGOS:** "the leaves of the tree were for the healing of the nations"
- **PNEUMA:** "the leaves of the tree are for the healing of the nations"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS, using "were" to maintain past-tense consistency with the vision narrative, though the Greek *eis therapeian* is a purpose construction.

### Verse 3
- **EMET:** "every curse will be no more"
- **LOGOS:** "there shall be no more curse"
- **PNEUMA:** "There will no longer be any curse"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. EMET's "every curse will be no more" is more literal (*pan katathema ouk estai eti*) but awkward in English. LOGOS restructures to natural English while preserving the meaning. PNEUMA's "any curse" is close but less formal.

### Verse 3 (continued)
- **EMET:** "his servants will serve him"
- **LOGOS:** "his servants shall serve him"
- **PNEUMA:** "his servants will worship him"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. The Greek *latreusousin* means "serve" in a cultic/worship sense. PNEUMA renders it "worship," which captures part of the meaning but loses the service dimension. "Serve" is the more comprehensive rendering.

### Verse 5
- **EMET:** "they have no need of light of lamp and light of sun"
- **LOGOS:** "they have no need of the light of a lamp and the light of the sun"
- **PNEUMA:** "They will need no lamp, no sun"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS, preserving the double "light of" construction present in Sinaiticus. PNEUMA drastically simplifies, losing the repeated *phōs* ("light of").

### Verse 5 (continued)
- **EMET:** "they will reign unto the ages of the ages"
- **LOGOS:** "they shall reign unto the ages of the ages"
- **PNEUMA:** "they will reign forever and ever"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Unto the ages of the ages" preserves the Semitic superlative *eis tous aionas ton aionon*, consistent with the decision in 20:10.

### Verse 6
- **EMET:** "These words [are] faithful and true; and the Lord, the God of the spirits of the prophets"
- **LOGOS:** "These words are faithful and true. And the Lord, the God of the spirits of the prophets"
- **PNEUMA:** "These words are trustworthy and true. The Lord, the God who inspires the prophets"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA's "trustworthy" replaces "faithful" (*pistoi*), and "who inspires the prophets" is a dynamic paraphrase of "of the spirits of the prophets" (*tōn pneumatōn tōn prophētōn*).

### Verse 6 (continued)
- **EMET:** "the things which must come to pass quickly"
- **LOGOS:** "the things which must come to pass shortly"
- **PNEUMA:** "what must happen soon"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Shortly" renders *en tachei* with appropriate formality. PNEUMA's "soon" and "happen" are more colloquial.

### Verse 7
- **EMET:** "behold, I am coming quickly"
- **LOGOS:** "behold, I am coming quickly"
- **PNEUMA:** "Look! I am coming soon!"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS. "Behold" preserves prophetic register. PNEUMA's "Look!" and "soon" with exclamation mark alter the tone.

### Verse 8
- **EMET:** "I, Yoannes, [am] the one hearing and seeing these things"
- **LOGOS:** "I, John, am the one hearing and seeing these things"
- **PNEUMA:** "I, John, am the one who heard and saw all of this"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. EMET's "Yoannes" is a transliteration not adopted for the consensus. PNEUMA shifts to past tense ("heard and saw") and adds "all of this," both departing from the Greek present participles *akouōn kai blepōn*.

### Verse 8 (continued)
- **EMET:** "I fell to worship before the feet of the angel showing me these things"
- **LOGOS:** "I fell down to worship before the feet of the angel who showed me these things"
- **PNEUMA:** "I fell at the feet of the angel who had shown me these things, ready to worship him"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "ready to worship him," an interpretive expansion.

### Verse 9
- **EMET:** "See [that you do] not! I am a fellow-servant of you and of your brothers the prophets"
- **LOGOS:** "See that you do not; I am a fellow servant of you and of your brothers the prophets"
- **PNEUMA:** "No! Do not do that! I am a fellow servant -- alongside you and your brothers the prophets"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA's "No! Do not do that!" is a dramatic expansion of the terse Greek *hora mē* ("See not!"). "Alongside" paraphrases the genitive construction.

### Verse 9 (continued)
- **EMET:** "those keeping the words of this book"
- **LOGOS:** "those who keep the words of this book"
- **PNEUMA:** "all who treasure the words of this book"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA's "treasure" intensifies "keep" (*tērountōn*) beyond what the Greek warrants, and adds "all."

### Verse 11
- **EMET:** "The one doing unrighteousness, let him do unrighteousness still; and the filthy one, let him be filthy still; and the righteous one, let him do righteousness still; and the holy one, let him be holy still"
- **LOGOS:** "Let the one who does wrong continue to do wrong, and let the filthy one continue to be filthy, and let the righteous one continue to practice righteousness, and let the holy one continue to be sanctified"
- **PNEUMA:** "Let the wrongdoer go on doing wrong. Let the filthy go on being filthy. Let the righteous go on doing right. Let the holy go on being holy"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS with one modification: "continue to be holy" rather than "continue to be sanctified." The Greek *hagiasthētō* is an aorist passive imperative that can mean "be made holy" or "be holy." "Be holy" (PNEUMA) is simpler; "be sanctified" (LOGOS) preserves the passive voice. SOPHIA adopts "be holy" for the consensus as it preserves the parallel structure with the other three clauses. EMET's word order is too literal for consensus English. PNEUMA's phrasing is overly simplified ("wrongdoer," "go on").

### Verse 12
- **EMET:** "Behold, I am coming quickly, and my reward [is] with me, to render to each one as his work is"
- **LOGOS:** "Behold, I am coming quickly, and my reward is with me, to render to each one as his work is"
- **PNEUMA:** "Look! I am coming soon, and I am bringing my reward with me. I will repay each person according to what they have done"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA's "bringing my reward" and "repay each person according to what they have done" are dynamic expansions.

### Verse 14
- **EMET:** "Blessed [are] those washing their robes, so that their authority will be over the tree of life and by the gates they may enter into the city"
- **LOGOS:** "Blessed are those who wash their robes, so that their authority shall be over the tree of life, and they may enter by the gates into the city"
- **PNEUMA:** "Blessed are those who wash their robes, so that they may have the right to the tree of life and may enter the city through its gates"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA's "the right to" replaces "authority over" (*exousia epi*) -- the Greek *exousia* specifically means "authority/power," and *epi* means "over/upon," not merely "to." EMET's word order ("by the gates they may enter") is too literal.

### Verse 15
- **EMET:** "the fornicators" / "everyone loving and doing falsehood"
- **LOGOS:** "the sexually immoral" / "everyone who loves and practices falsehood"
- **PNEUMA:** "the sexually immoral" / "everyone who loves and lives a lie"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Sexually immoral" is preferred over archaic "fornicators." PNEUMA's "lives a lie" is a dynamic rendering of *poiōn pseudos* ("doing/practicing falsehood").

### Verse 16
- **EMET:** "I, Yeshua, sent my angel to testify to you these things for the churches. I am the root and the offspring of David, the bright morning star"
- **LOGOS:** "I, Jesus, sent my angel to testify these things to you for the churches. I am the root and the offspring of David, the bright morning star"
- **PNEUMA:** "I, Jesus, have sent my angel to give you this testimony for the churches. I am the Root and the Descendant of David -- the bright Morning Star"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. EMET's "Yeshua" is not adopted for the consensus. PNEUMA capitalizes "Root," "Descendant," and "Morning Star" as titles, which is interpretive. "Offspring" is preferred over PNEUMA's "Descendant" for *genos*, which means "race/stock/offspring."

### Verse 17
- **EMET:** "the one hearing, let him say, 'Come.' And the one thirsting, let him come; the one desiring, let him take [the] water of life freely"
- **LOGOS:** "let the one who hears say, 'Come.' And let the one who thirsts come; let the one who desires take the water of life freely"
- **PNEUMA:** "Let everyone who hears this say, 'Come!' Let the thirsty come. Let anyone who desires it take the water of life as a free gift"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "everyone," "this," "anyone," and renders *dōrean* ("freely") as "as a free gift" -- an expansion. EMET's construction is too literal.

### Verse 18
- **EMET:** "I testify to everyone hearing the words of the prophecy of this book: if anyone adds upon them, God will add upon him the plagues having been written in this book"
- **LOGOS:** "I testify to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy of this book: if anyone adds to them, God shall add to him the plagues that are written in this book"
- **PNEUMA:** "I give this solemn warning to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy of this book: if anyone adds anything to them, God will add to that person the plagues described in this book"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA's "solemn warning" interprets *marturō* ("I testify") and "described" replaces "written" (*gegrammenas*).

### Verse 19
- **EMET:** "God will take away his part from the tree of life and out of the holy city, the things having been written in this book"
- **LOGOS:** "God shall take away his part from the tree of life and out of the holy city, the things which are written in this book"
- **PNEUMA:** "God will take away that person's share in the tree of life and in the holy city -- the very things described in this book"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA's "share in" softens "part from" (*meros apo*), and "described" again replaces "written."

### Verse 20
- **EMET:** "The one testifying these things says, 'Yes, I am coming quickly.' Amen. Come, Lord Yeshua."
- **LOGOS:** "The one who testifies these things says, 'Yes, I am coming quickly.' Amen. Come, Lord Jesus."
- **PNEUMA:** "He who testifies to all of this says, 'Yes! I am coming soon!' Amen! Come, Lord Jesus!"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. EMET's "Yeshua" is not adopted. PNEUMA adds exclamation marks, "all of this," and "soon" for *tachu* ("quickly"). The restrained punctuation of LOGOS better preserves the solemnity of the Bible's final prayer.

### Verse 21
- **EMET:** "The grace of the Lord Yeshua [be] with all the holy ones. Amen."
- **LOGOS:** "The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with all the saints. Amen."
- **PNEUMA:** "The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all of God's people. Amen."
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA renders: "The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all the saints. Amen." EMET's "Yeshua" and "holy ones" are not adopted. LOGOS adds "Christ" -- some Sinaiticus readings include *Christou* but the primary reading is *Iesou* without *Christou*; SOPHIA follows the shorter reading. PNEUMA's "God's people" replaces "saints" (*hagiōn*), losing the technical term. "Saints" (LOGOS) is the standard formal rendering of *hagioi*.

**Final note from SOPHIA:** This is the last verse of the last book of the Bible. The Codex Sinaiticus closes here with AMHN and the colophon APOKALYPSIS IOANNOU. The translation of the Christian Scriptures, from the opening of Matthew to this closing benediction, is complete. The Bible ends as it ought -- with grace, extended to all who are called holy, sealed with the most ancient of affirmations: Amen.

--- SOPHIA
